properties.remark: GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL FELL ALONG WYOMING BOULEVARD ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF CASPER FROM 1500 TO 1513 HOURS.
CAR WAS LIFTED AND SURROUNDED BY DEBRIS ON WYOMING BOULEVARD BETWEEN CASPER MOUNTAIN ROAD AND MCKINLEY.
10 TO 15 MINUTES IN DURATION OF 1 INCH DIAMETER HAIL

*** 1 INJ *** BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN IN MIDDLE OF DUBOIS. DAMAGE TO RESTROOM FACILITY ROOF AND 8X8 INCH SIGN POST OF LOCAL BUSINESS. DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH TORNADO, NOT MICROBURST, AND CONFINED TO LESS THAN 1 SQUARE BLOCK. 1 INJURY FROM BROKEN WINDOWN GLASS. RATED EF0.
HIGHWAY PATROL EAST OF KIRBY SPOTTED A TORNADO.
REPORTED BY TRUCKER THROUGH JOHNSON COUNTY S.O.
PUBLIC REPORTED A TORNADO ALONG INTERSTATE 25 JUST NORTH OF BARR NUNN.
HAIL WAS REPORTED AT ORANGE VIEW ROAD AT THE MIDWEST TURN OFF FROM INTERSTATE 25.
AT BITTER CREEK HIGHWAY OBSERVATION SITE.
REPORTED BY MOTORIST THROUGH NATRONA COUNTY S.O.
NICKEL TO QUARTER SIZED HAIL ACCUMUALTED 3 TO 4 INCHES AT JENNY LAKE IN GRAND TETON NATIONAL PARK. ONE PERSON RECIEVED LACERATIONS...SEVERAL OTHERS HAD WELTS.
STREET FLOODING WITH ONE FOOT DEEP WATER. ONE BASEMENT FLOODED ON SOLLIDAY STREET.
